leadText: Hyper Voice (Japanese: ハイパーボイス Hyper Voice) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation III. It is: TR42 in Pokémon Sword and Shield TM117 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM071 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation III-V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation IX 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Hyper Voice inflicts damage to all adjacent opponents. Hyper Voice is a sound-based move . Generation III-V [ edit | edit source ] If used on a Pokémon behind a substitute , it hits that Pokémon's substitute. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Hyper Voice can now hit Pokémon even if they are behind a substitute. Hyper Voice will destroy certain natural objects found in the background of battles . Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 90 108 Cooldown 8s Move wind-up time 15 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description R S E Colo XD A loud attack that uses sound waves to injure. FR LG The user lets loose a horribly loud shout with the power to damage. D P Pt HG SS PBR B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA The user lets loose a horribly echoing shout with the power to inflict damage. S V The user attacks by letting loose a horribly loud, resounding cry. ZA The user attacks by unleashing horribly loud, resounding vibrations.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる Conq. The user lets loose a horribly echoing shout with the power to inflict damage. MD GtI It damages enemies. S MD It damages nearby enemies.
